response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack/web engineer with strong testing and software craftsmanship skills, but the role requires a hands-on C++ specialist with industrial automation, secure coding (IEC 62443), and message-broker/IoT expertise. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago; the rest of the career is JavaScript/TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of MQTT, OPC UA, industrial cybersecurity standards, or multi-threaded C++ systems work.
strengths: 15+ years of overall software engineering experience with strong TDD/CI-CD craftsmanship
Background in low-latency, high-throughput services and distributed systems (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Demonstrated mentoring, workshops, and technical communication skills
concerns: C/C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014-2015) and not recent
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or related protocols
Primary stack is web/JS/TS/DLang, misaligned with a hands-on C++ platform role

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent, substantial C++/STL experience
No industrial automation or IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general engineering seniority and systems experience
